Credentials

Certifications and learning track (2024)

A record of how I invested in product, mobile, and software fundamentals while growing in business roles.

Home / Journal / Credentials / Certifications and Learning Track
Credentials Published February 27, 2026 By Thomas Hayford

In 2024, I decided to formalize some of the technical and product knowledge I was using in my daily work. My goal was simple: understand systems better so I can support businesses more effectively.

I completed three certifications that gave me stronger language for product conversations, clearer understanding of software delivery, and better context for mobile-led business growth.

  • Get Started with Android Development (SkillUp EdTech) Verification link
  • Introduction to Software Engineering (IBM) Verification link
  • Introduction to Mobile App Development (IBM) Verification link

These courses did not change my identity into a developer. They strengthened my ability to work with product teams, communicate clearly with technical stakeholders, and make better decisions in relationship and operations roles.

I see this learning track as part of the same journey: helping businesses grow with better systems, better communication, and better execution quality.

I also documented additional 2025 learning milestones from my authored LinkedIn activity, including GIS-focused progress and other technical foundations. You can read that update on the LinkedIn learning highlights page.

Credential Summary

  • Completed3 certifications in 2024.
  • Focus AreasSoftware engineering fundamentals, Android basics, mobile development.
  • UsageImproved cross-functional communication in business and growth work.
More credential notes LinkedIn learning highlights
